title: Indulgent Tender Indian Sirloin Steak Sushi
description: A unique fusion dish combining the tenderness of Indian sirloin steak with the delicacy of sushi, featuring Kerala-inspired flavors and shojin-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the sirloin steak with garam masala, curry powder, and northern indian spices.
Heat sesame oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and sear the steak for 2-3 minutes per side.
Finish cooking the steak in the oven to an internal temperature of 130 degrees Fahrenheit for medium-rare.
Let the steak rest for 10 minutes before slicing it into thin strips.
Prepare the sushi rice according to the package instructions and allow it to cool.
Cut the nori sheets into smaller pieces and shape the cooled sushi rice into nigiri shapes.
Place a slice of the steak onto each nigiri shape and top with a small amount of wasabi paste and pickled ginger if desired.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
